items.title: Buying a unit is cheaper than renting in this inner-city spot
items.summary: New research suggests Australians could save hundreds per fortnight by buying rather than renting a unit in certain places.

items.title: Lebanon at 'breaking point', UN warns, as displacement crisis grows
items.summary: Lebanon's civilians bear the brunt as fighting spreads, triggering mass displacement and growing international concern.

items.title: Australian officials pulled from Lebanon, Israel and UAE amid war in the Middle East
items.summary: Non-essential government staff were told to get out of Israel and the UAE earlier on Thursday.

items.title: Their journey has just 'begun': Concerns Iranian players monitored by regime 'agents'
items.summary: Some are worried about the safety of Iranian players who sought asylum in Australia, raising concerns Iran could keep a close eye on them.

items.title: Qantas agrees to $105 million settlement over COVID-19 flight credits
items.summary: The airlines makes no admission of liability under the terms of the settlement, which still requires Federal Court approval.

items.title: Government's 100-million-litre fuel promise amid supply 'panic'
items.summary: The government has released additional fuel into the domestic market, reassuring Australians that "stocks remain very strong".

items.title: Suspect shot dead by security after ramming truck through synagogue in Michigan
items.summary: None of the synagogue's staff or the children at its early childhood centre were injured.

items.title: Iran vows to keep Strait of Hormuz shut in first statement from new Supreme Leader
items.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei says the leverage of closing the Strait of Hormuz should be used and that attacks on Gulf countries will continue.

items.title: Matildas v North Korea: Will this Asian Cup quarter-final see Kerr re-enact first goal magic?
items.summary: With the fate of two players unknown, will the Matildas be able to secure their spot in the 2027 Women's World Cup?

items.title: Trump says Iran at the World Cup wouldn't be 'appropriate'. What would happen if they pull out?
items.summary: If Iran pulls out of the FIFA World Cup 2026, a replacement may have to be found.

items.title: First people arrested under new Queensland laws banning 'from the river to the sea' chant
items.summary: The Queensland government is under fire from activists who consider the hate speech laws to be "hallmarks of an authoritarian police state".

items.title: Two bodies found in Queensland floods are those of missing backpackers, police say
items.summary: The first flood-related deaths in the rain-battered region has devastated the community of Gympie, where the bodies were found.

items.title: I'm a scientist. One conversation led me to rethink climate change
items.summary: As a teenager growing up in a rural town, Jacob Martin was sceptical about climate change. Some pivotal moments in his life changed that.

items.title: Before offering refuge to Iranian players, Australia took in another team
items.summary: Several members of the Iranian football team have chosen asylum in Australia. But it isn't the first time the country has stepped in.

items.title: Iran warns of US$200 oil barrels as it retaliates against US-Israeli bombardment
items.summary: A spokesperson for Iran's military command warned "the oil price depends on regional security".

items.title: 'Hypocritical': Government moves to curb some Iranian visas after footballer asylum cases
items.summary: Critics accuse the government of concealing the "true effect" of the changes, arguing they would prevent people from seeking refuge.

items.title: Former ASIO chief says he quit antisemitism royal commission as he felt 'surplus', was 'overpaid'
items.summary: Dennis Richardson's sudden departure comes ahead of the royal commission's interim report being delivered next month.

items.title: Matt Canavan calls for 'hyper Australia' with 'more babies' as he leads National Party
items.summary: The party chose its new leader in a 45-minute meeting, following David Littleproud's surprise resignation on Tuesday.

items.title: Why it's hard to predict just how much airfares will rise amid War in the Middle East
items.summary: Qantas has flagged fare increases, citing aviation fuel costs which have soared up to 150 per cent since since the start of the conflict.

items.title: Robodebt inquiry clears Scott Morrison but finds two others corrupt
items.summary: A watchdog's probe into officials involved in the robodebt scheme found two people carried out corrupt conduct.

items.title: Scouring the countryside for 600L of fuel: Farmer Rob has 'never seen' anything like this
items.summary: Farmers warn supply disruptions could threaten Australia's food production.

items.title: Qantas hikes international fares as war sends fuel costs soaring
items.summary: Despite the war, high demand for international flights has seen cheaper fares selling much faster than usual.

items.title: 'Patchwork' disaster response criticised as Aboriginal communities submerged in rising floodwaters
items.summary: The NT's peak Aboriginal legal body has put their emergency measures to the Commonwealth and Territory government as Minister McCarthy called out the lack of mainstream media flood coverage on national television.

items.title: Sonja asked high schoolers to debate about tradwives — and received international backlash
items.summary: In Australia, are terms such as "free speech" and "cultural safety" now used in place of constructive, respectful debate?
